expectations.text: Defends the heatmap or recommends small-multiples cohort retention curves (not a 3D surface, not a pie chart, not a single averaged line)
expectations.evidence: 'A retention curves overlay (small set of cohorts as lines, x = months since signup) as the hero chart.' Also mentions 'Small multiples (4 rows x 6 cols of mini line charts, one per cohort)' as a variation.

expectations.text: Explicitly addresses how to handle the triangular shape / missing cells (greyed out, NaN, diagonal cutoff)
expectations.evidence: 'Leave the lower triangle truly empty (no gray fill, no "N/A"). The diagonal edge is information.' and 'The triangular structure disappears entirely because every cohort starts at month 0.'

expectations.text: Reframes 'polish' as data presentation craft (typography, spacing, color scale) rather than chartjunk/decoration
expectations.evidence: 'Treat it as a table first, color second â€” this is the Tufte-style move and what designers usually mean by "polished"' followed by guidance on tabular numerals, right-aligned numbers, narrow color range, no gridlines.

expectations.text: Specifies a sensible color scale for retention â€” sequential or diverging anchored on a target, NOT rainbow
expectations.evidence: 'Use a muted, narrow-range color scale (e.g., a single hue from white to a brand color, mapped to the 60â€“100% range, not 0â€“100%). Don't use red-green diverging' and 'Don't use viridis on a board deck.'

expectations.text: Provides at least one concrete actionable next step (revised chart description, code, or design spec)
expectations.evidence: Concrete slide structure: 'Hero chart: Option A (retention curves overlay), one sentence headline... Reference table: Option B (triangular table-heatmap)... Footnote: cohort definition...'
